reasoning: The market logic is an 'if and only if' between a ceasefire before 2026 and Trump winning the 2025 Nobel Peace Prize. The background information states the Nobel market has already resolved NO (Trump did not win the prize). This means the market simplifies to resolving YES if and only if the ceasefire market resolves NO (i.e., NO bilateral ceasefire before 2026). As of October 26, 2025, there are only about two months remaining in the year. A major bilateral ceasefire in a protracted and intense conflict like the Russia-Ukraine war is highly unlikely to happen suddenly within two months without prior substantial progress (which would likely have been factored into Nobel considerations if brokered by the US). Given the short time horizon remaining and base rates for conflict termination, a ceasefire is unlikely. Therefore, the probability of the ceasefire market resolving NO is high, making the probability of this market resolving YES correspondingly high. I estimate an ~84% chance that there will be no ceasefire by year's end.
